notes.note: The consuls of 49 BC, C. Claudius Marcellus and L. Cornelius Lentulus Crus, charged with raising and leading the Pompeian levy in Italy; Cicero's contempt is for their failure to muster any real force against Caesar.

notes.anchor: on the day before the Nones
notes.note: The Romans counted dates backward from three fixed points each month: the Kalends (1st), the Nones (5th or 7th), and the Ides (13th or 15th). The day before the Nones of February (Nones = 5th) is 4 February.

notes.anchor: the cohorts of the Appian legions
notes.note: Two legions handed over to Pompey by Caesar in 50 BC under the pretext of a Parthian war (named for the consul Ap. Claudius or for the road by which they came); their loyalty to Pompey was doubtful, having served long under Caesar.
Cicero weighs which tyrant Caesar will resemble: Phalaris of Acragas, byword for cruelty (the brazen bull), or Pisistratus of Athens, the comparatively mild usurper who kept the city's laws.
